channel.title: Gas-to-Energy on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in Gas-to-Energy on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Sees It Differently – Thursday, April 9, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reviews the week's news and finds the government, as usual, doing everything right despite what the papers say.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Reports — Tuesday, April 7, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh is very proud of CARIFTA and the 60th Independence celebrations. He has some thoughts on Lindsayca but they are measured.

item.title: The Daily Brief — Tuesday, April 7, 2026
item.description: Lindsayca's executives fly private while Guyana sits in the dark. CARIFTA ends with six medals. A Chinese acrobatic troupe is coming. Also: flood fears.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Reports — Monday, April 6, 2026
item.description: Uncle Ramesh, calling from Queens, is very impressed with the airstrip and not worried about the river.

item.title: The Daily Brief — Monday, April 6, 2026
item.description: Suriname charges up a storm, Karasabai gets an airstrip, fishermen still missing, and Guyana's athletes have a tough day in Grenada.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: De Brief Conveniently Forget Who Actually Building This Country
item.description: Uncle Ramesh has some thoughts about handrails, fuel prices, and why Guyana getting a field hospital in Essequibo is actually a big deal.

item.title: Monday Brief: Guyana Lectures the Caribbean on Climate, Then Drowns
item.description: Guyana told the Caribbean to climate-proof their infrastructure, then spent the weekend underwater. Also: Guyoil raised gas prices, the AFC says the 50% profit share is a fantasy, and we got the handrails on the US$6.8B FPSO.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh: De Brief Forget To Mention All De GOOD Tings Happening!
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads de Sunday Brief and has some thoughts about what dem left out. As usual.

item.title: Sunday Brief: Georgetown Flood, Karpowership Extension & The Streets That Used To Be Ours
item.description: Georgetown flooded again, the powerships are staying longer than expected, the government and the Mayor are fighting over who owns the streets, and Rihanna's makeup is now available in Guyana. Sunday, bai.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Responds: 'ONE Point? That's PROGRESS, Beta!'
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Queens celebrates the corruption index improvement, explains why 30 blacklisted contractors proves the government is WORKING, and has strong opinions about the Guyana flag at the Super Bowl.

item.title: Wednesday's Guyana Brief - Corruption Score: We Moved ONE Point, 30 Contractors Blacklisted, and the Flag That Started a War
item.description: Guyana crawls up ONE spot on the corruption index, 30 contractors get blacklisted, Ali tours Brazil’s gas plant, sugar promises continue, and the Guyana flag at the Super Bowl has people HEATED.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Take: Two Guyanese Boys Own de World Cup, But de Brief Want Talk About Auditor General
item.description: Uncle Ramesh from Queens celebrates Hetmyer and Shepherd’s World Cup heroics, praises the Bayroc Stadium opening, and wonders why the Brief is obsessed with Christopher Ram.

item.title: 🇬🇾 Saturday Brief: Opposition Leader Rips Budget, Secret Extradition Revealed, and Linden Gets a Stadium
item.description: Opposition Leader Mohamed delivers maiden budget speech and it’s a SCORCHER, a secret US extradition request surfaces, Linden opens its shiny new stadium, Ali promises miners the moon (if they declare their gold), and the Windies take on Scotland in the T20 World Cup opener.

item.title: Progress Report: Linden-Mabura Road at 62%, Gas-to-Energy Deadlines Extended, Bayrock Stadium Opens Jan 31
item.description: Tracking what the government promised vs what’s actually happening. This week: Linden-Mabura road hits 62%, gas plant deadlines pushed back (again), and Linden finally getting its stadium.
